race.comment: GOLDEN CLOUD (5) caught the eye on debut when rattling home with some of the meeting's fastest splits. If Toeroek can have him that touch closer at the bend, he'll be mighty hard to hold out over the longer trip. WHISKEY TUMBLER (7) comes through the same maiden, where he was well supported on debut off sharp hit-outs. He was only collared late by a much fitter horse and, with a map advantage over GOLDEN CLOUD, is capable of going one better. HARRINO (1) has a slight fitness edge and is building the right way. On the quick back-up he gets his chance to take closer order and is one of the key hopes. BRAVE FORTUNE (4) is a Hanseatic gelding on debut for Will Clarken who has progressed nicely through his lead-up work. Worth monitoring. TIPTOP TORI (12) resumes for the Gluyas camp and, while she's likely looking for further, this is a suitable starting point. Not dismissing her lightly.
